name: Chicken Kabab Recipe
short_description: Juicy and flavorful chicken kababs, perfect for grilling or pan-frying.
full_description: Experience the authentic taste of Indian chicken kababs with Qureshi's special blend. These kababs are marinated to perfection and can be grilled, baked, or pan-fried for a delicious meal.

ingredients.text: 1 tsp red chili powder (adjust to taste)
Onion rings, lemon wedges, mint chutney for serving

preparation_steps.text: In a large bowl, combine yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, Qureshi's Chicken Kabab Masala, red chili powder, and salt. Mix well.
Add chicken cubes to the marinade, ensuring they are well coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, preferably overnight.
If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for 30 minutes to prevent burning.
Preheat grill to medium-high heat, or oven to 200°C (400°F), or a non-stick pan over medium heat.

cooking_tips: Marinate chicken for at least 2 hours for best flavor and tenderness.
Do not overcrowd the grill/pan; cook in batches if necessary.
Brush with oil or butter during grilling/cooking to keep kababs moist.
For a smoky flavor, you can add a piece of charcoal to the marinade while resting.
serving_suggestions: Serve hot with onion rings, lemon wedges, and fresh mint chutney.
Pairs well with naan, roti, or a simple green salad.

name: Green Chicken Kabab Recipe
short_description: Tender chicken kababs with a vibrant green marinade of fresh herbs and spices.
full_description: These visually appealing and delicious green chicken kababs are made with a unique blend of fresh herbs and Qureshi's Green Chicken Kabab Masala, offering a refreshing twist on traditional kababs.

ingredients.text: 2 tbsp Qureshi's Green Chicken Kabab Masala
Lemon wedges and fresh mint leaves for garnish

preparation_steps.text: In a blender, make a smooth paste of coriander leaves, mint leaves, green chilies, and a little water if needed.
In a large bowl, combine yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, Qureshi's Green Chicken Kabab Masala, the herb paste, and salt. Mix well.
Add chicken cubes to the marinade, ensuring they are well coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, preferably overnight.
Thread marinated chicken onto skewers. Preheat grill/oven/pan as per cooking method.

cooking_tips: For a brighter green color, blanch the mint and coriander leaves before blending.
Adjust green chilies to your spice preference.
Grill until lightly charred for extra flavor.
serving_suggestions: Serve hot with a side of raita (yogurt dip) or a simple salad.
Garnish with fresh lemon wedges and mint leaves.

name: Pepper Chicken Recipe
short_description: A spicy and aromatic dry chicken dish, rich with black pepper flavor.
full_description: Prepare this quick and easy Pepper Chicken using Qureshi's Pepper Masala for a burst of spicy, peppery goodness. It's a perfect side dish or appetizer.

ingredients.text: 500g boneless chicken, cut into small pieces
Fresh coriander leaves, chopped, for garnish

preparation_steps.text: Heat oil in a pan. Add sliced onions and sauté until translucent.
Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies (if using), sauté for a minute until fragrant.
Add chicken pieces and turmeric powder. Cook until chicken is lightly browned and juices run clear.
Stir in Qureshi's Pepper Masala and bell pepper slices. Cook for 5-7 minutes until chicken is cooked through and masala is well combined.
Add curry leaves if desired and cook for another minute.

cooking_tips: For extra heat, increase the amount of green chilies or black pepper.
Ensure chicken is cut into small, even pieces for quick and even cooking.
Serve immediately to enjoy the crisp texture of bell peppers.
serving_suggestions: Serve hot as a starter or with plain rice, roti, or naan.
Garnish with fresh coriander leaves.

name: Pepper Mutton Recipe
short_description: A rich and robust dry mutton dish, infused with the bold flavor of black pepper.
full_description: Indulge in this intensely flavored Pepper Mutton prepared with Qureshi's Pepper Masala. It’s a perfect dish for those who love strong, spicy flavors and tender mutton.

ingredients.text: Fresh coriander leaves, chopped, for garnish

preparation_steps.text: Heat oil in a pressure cooker or heavy-bottomed pan. Add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies, sauté for a minute until fragrant.
Add mutton pieces and turmeric powder. Sauté until mutton is sealed and changes color.

Stir in Qureshi's Pepper Masala and salt. Mix well. Add about 1/2 cup of water, cover, and cook until mutton is tender (approx. 20-30 minutes in a pressure cooker after first whistle, or longer in a pan).
Once mutton is tender, if there's excess water, cook on high heat until it evaporates and the masala coats the mutton well.
Add curry leaves if desired and cook for another minute.

cooking_tips: Pressure cooking the mutton ensures tenderness and reduces cooking time.
Adjust the quantity of Qureshi's Pepper Masala to control the spice level.
For a richer flavor, marinate the mutton in yogurt and ginger-garlic paste for an hour before cooking.
serving_suggestions: Serve hot as a side dish with paratha, roti, or appam.
Garnish with fresh coriander leaves.

name: Mutton Masala Curry Recipe
short_description: A classic Indian mutton curry, slow-cooked to perfection with aromatic spices.
full_description: Savor the rich and robust flavors of this traditional Mutton Masala Curry. Prepared with Qureshi's Mutton Masala, this dish features tender mutton pieces in a thick, spicy gravy.

ingredients.text: Fresh coriander leaves, chopped, for garnish

preparation_steps.text: Marinate mutton with half of the ginger-garlic paste, yogurt, and a pinch of salt for 30 minutes.
Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pan or pressure cooker. Add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add the remaining ginger-garlic paste and sauté for a minute.
Add pureed tomatoes and cook until the oil separates.
Stir in Qureshi's Mutton Masala and marinated mutton. Sauté for 10-15 minutes until mutton is well browned.
Add 1-1.5 cups of water, cover, and cook until mutton is tender (approx. 45-60 minutes in a pan or 20-25 minutes in a pressure cooker after first whistle).
Adjust seasoning and consistency. Garnish with fresh coriander.

cooking_tips: Slow cooking mutton on low heat yields the most tender results.
For a richer gravy, add a tablespoon of cashew paste towards the end of cooking.
Always brown the onions well for a deep color and flavor in the curry.
serving_suggestions: Serve hot with steamed basmati rice, naan, or tandoori roti.
A side of sliced onions and lemon wedges complements the curry well.

name: Chicken Masala Curry Recipe
short_description: A hearty and flavorful chicken curry, a staple in Indian households.
full_description: This Chicken Masala Curry, made with Qureshi's Chicken Masala, is a comforting and flavorful dish. It's easy to prepare and perfect for a family meal.

ingredients.text: Fresh coriander leaves, chopped, for garnish

preparation_steps.text: Heat oil in a pan. Add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté for a minute.
Add chicken pieces and cook until they change color.
Stir in Qureshi's Chicken Masala, chopped tomatoes (and yogurt if using). Cook until oil separates from the masala.
Add 1 cup of water and salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until chicken is tender.
Garnish with fresh coriander leaves before serving.

cooking_tips: For a thicker gravy, cook uncovered for the last 5-10 minutes.
Marinating chicken in yogurt and spices for 30 minutes can enhance flavor and tenderness.
Adjust water quantity to achieve desired gravy consistency.
serving_suggestions: Best served with hot rice, roti, or paratha.
A simple cucumber salad can complement the curry well.

name: Veg Korma Recipe
short_description: A rich and creamy vegetable korma, delicately spiced and full of flavor.
full_description: Indulge in this luxurious Veg Korma, a perfect blend of fresh vegetables and Qureshi's Korma Masala. Its mild, creamy gravy makes it a family favorite.

ingredients.text: 1 cup mixed vegetables (carrots, peas, potatoes, beans), chopped
1/2 cup cashew paste (soaked cashews blended with water)
Fresh coriander leaves, chopped, for garnish

preparation_steps.text: Blanch mixed vegetables until half cooked. Set aside.
Heat oil in a pan. Add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté for a minute.
Stir in Qureshi's Korma Masala and cook for 2 minutes, adding a splash of water if needed.
Add blanched vegetables, cashew paste, and salt. Mix well.
Add 1/2 cup of water, cover, and simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until vegetables are fully cooked and gravy thickens.
Stir in cream or coconut milk. Cook for another 2-3 minutes. Garnish with fresh coriander.

cooking_tips: Use fresh, seasonal vegetables for the best flavor.
Adjust cream/coconut milk quantity for desired richness and consistency.
Soak cashews in hot water for 20 minutes before blending for a smoother paste.
serving_suggestions: Serve hot with naan, roti, or jeera rice.
A sprinkle of nuts or fried onions can add extra texture.

name: Chicken/Mutton Korma Recipe
short_description: A royal and aromatic Korma with tender chicken or mutton pieces in a rich, creamy gravy.
full_description: Experience the richness of Mughlai cuisine with this Chicken/Mutton Korma recipe, featuring tender meat cooked in a creamy, flavorful gravy with Qureshi's Korma Masala. Perfect for special occasions.

ingredients.text: 500g chicken or mutton, cut into pieces
2 large onions, thinly sliced and fried until golden brown
Fresh coriander leaves, chopped, for garnish

preparation_steps.text: Blend the fried onions with a little water to make a smooth paste. Set aside.
In a bowl, marinate chicken/mutton with ginger-garlic paste, yogurt, and salt for 30 minutes.
Heat oil/ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan. Add marinated meat and sauté until lightly browned.
Add Qureshi's Korma Masala and onion paste. Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring continuously.
Add 1 cup of water, cover, and cook until meat is tender (approx. 20-25 mins for chicken, 40-50 mins for mutton).
Stir in cashew-almond paste and simmer for another 5 minutes until gravy thickens. Garnish with fresh coriander.

cooking_tips: For best results, use a mix of cashews and almonds for the paste.
Always fry onions until deeply golden for the characteristic korma flavor.
Adjust consistency by adding more water or simmering longer.
serving_suggestions: Serve with butter naan, paratha, or saffron-infused basmati rice.
A sprinkle of fried cashews can elevate the presentation.

name: Veg Biryani Recipe
short_description: A fragrant and aromatic layered rice dish with mixed vegetables and spices.
full_description: Experience the magic of layered flavors with this delightful Veg Biryani, made effortless with Qureshi's Biryani Masala. A perfect one-pot meal for vegetarians.

ingredients.text: 1 cup basmati rice, soaked for 30 mins
1.5 cups mixed vegetables (carrots, peas, potatoes, cauliflower), chopped
1 large onion, thinly sliced and fried until golden brown
Fresh mint and coriander leaves, chopped
Saffron strands soaked in warm milk (optional)

preparation_steps.text: Cook basmati rice until 70% done. Drain and set aside.
In a bowl, marinate mixed vegetables with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, Qureshi's Biryani Masala, and salt for 30 minutes.
Heat oil/ghee in a heavy-bottomed pot. Add half of the fried onions, mint, and coriander leaves. Add marinated vegetables and cook for 10 minutes.
Layer half of the partially cooked rice over the vegetables. Top with remaining fried onions, mint, and coriander.
Layer the remaining rice, saffron milk (if using). Cover tightly and cook on low heat (dum) for 20-25 minutes.
Let it rest for 10 minutes before gently fluffing and serving.

cooking_tips: Do not overcook the rice in the initial step; it should be firm.
Use a heavy-bottomed pot and seal tightly for effective dum cooking.
For best flavor, use good quality basmati rice.
serving_suggestions: Serve hot with raita (yogurt dip), mirchi ka salan, or a simple green salad.
Garnish with fried onions and fresh coriander.

name: Chicken/Mutton Biryani Recipe
short_description: A traditional layered biryani with tender chicken or mutton and aromatic spices.
full_description: Master the art of biryani with this Chicken/Mutton Biryani recipe. Qureshi's Biryani Masala ensures a perfectly balanced, flavorful, and aromatic dish that's a true feast.

ingredients.text: 1 cup basmati rice, soaked for 30 mins
500g chicken or mutton, cut into pieces
1 large onion, thinly sliced and fried until golden brown
Fresh mint and coriander leaves, chopped
Saffron strands soaked in warm milk (optional)

preparation_steps.text: Cook basmati rice until 70% done. Drain and set aside.
Marinate chicken/mutton with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, half of the fried onions, mint, coriander, Qureshi's Biryani Masala, and salt for 1-2 hours.
Heat oil/ghee in a heavy-bottomed pot. Add marinated meat and cook for 10-15 minutes on medium heat.
Layer half of the partially cooked rice over the meat. Top with remaining fried onions, mint, and coriander.
Layer the remaining rice, saffron milk (if using). Cover tightly and cook on low heat (dum) for 25-35 minutes (chicken) or 40-50 minutes (mutton).
Let it rest for 10-15 minutes before gently fluffing and serving.

cooking_tips: Ensure meat is well marinated for tender and flavorful biryani.
Seal the pot properly during dum cooking to retain moisture and aroma.
Adjust spice levels by controlling the amount of Qureshi's Biryani Masala.
serving_suggestions: Serve hot with raita, salan, and a fresh salad.
Garnish with fried onions, fresh coriander, and a boiled egg.

name: Crispy Fish Fry Recipe
short_description: Perfectly spiced and crispy fried fish, a delightful seafood appetizer.
full_description: Achieve restaurant-style crispy fish fry at home with Qureshi's Fish Fry Masala. This recipe ensures a crunchy exterior and tender, flavorful fish inside.

ingredients.text: 500g fish fillets or steaks (e.g., Kingfish, Pomfret), cleaned
Lemon wedges and onion slices for serving

preparation_steps.text: Pat the fish dry thoroughly with paper towels. Make shallow slits on the fish fillets/steaks.
In a bowl, mix Qureshi's Fish Fry Masala, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, turmeric powder, and salt to form a thick paste.
Apply the marinade evenly on the fish, ensuring it gets into the slits. Marinate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 1 hour in the refrigerator.
Heat oil in a deep pan for frying. Once hot, carefully place marinated fish pieces (do not overcrowd).
Fry for 3-5 minutes on each side until golden brown and crispy. Ensure fish is cooked through.
Remove from oil and place on absorbent paper to drain excess oil.

cooking_tips: Ensure fish is completely dry before marinating for a crispier fry.
Do not overcook the fish, as it can become dry.
Maintain a consistent oil temperature for even cooking and crispiness.
serving_suggestions: Serve hot with fresh lemon wedges, onion slices, and a sprinkle of chaat masala.
Pairs well with a simple tomato ketchup or mint chutney.

name: Traditional Fish Curry Recipe
short_description: A tangy and spicy South Indian style fish curry, rich with coastal flavors.
full_description: Dive into the authentic flavors of the coast with this Traditional Fish Curry. Made with Qureshi's Fish Curry Masala, this dish is packed with tangy, spicy, and aromatic notes, perfect with rice.

ingredients.text: 500g firm white fish (e.g., Kingfish, Pomfret, Mackerel), cut into steaks
Fresh curry leaves and green chilies for tempering

preparation_steps.text: Clean and wash fish pieces. Marinate with a pinch of salt and turmeric for 15 minutes.
Heat oil in a pan. Add curry leaves and green chilies, then chopped onions and sauté until translucent.
Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté for a minute. Then add pureed tomatoes and cook until oil separates.
Stir in Qureshi's Fish Curry Masala and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add tamarind extract, 1 cup of water, and salt. Bring to a boil, then add fish pieces. Cover and simmer for 10-12 minutes, or until fish is cooked through.
If using, stir in coconut milk and simmer for another 2 minutes. Do not boil after adding coconut milk.

cooking_tips: For a truly authentic flavor, use freshly ground coconut paste instead of coconut milk.
Do not stir the curry too vigorously after adding fish to prevent breaking the pieces.
Adjust tamarind quantity for desired tanginess.
serving_suggestions: Serve hot with steamed rice, particularly red rice or idiyappam.
A side of simple vegetable stir-fry complements the curry.

name: Barbecue Chicken Recipe
short_description: Smoky and tender chicken, grilled to perfection with a rich barbecue marinade.
full_description: Fire up your grill for this delicious Barbecue Chicken, marinated with Qureshi's Chicken Barbecue Masala. It's an ideal dish for outdoor cooking or a flavorful weeknight dinner.

ingredients.text: 500g chicken drumsticks or thighs, skin on or off

preparation_steps.text: Make deep slits on the chicken pieces. In a bowl, combine yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, Qureshi's Chicken Barbecue Masala, olive oil, and salt.
Add chicken to the marinade, ensuring each piece is thoroughly coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight.
Preheat grill to medium-high heat. Lightly oil the grill grates.
Place chicken on the hot grill. Cook for 25-30 minutes, turning occasionally, until chicken is cooked through, juices run clear, and skin is nicely charred.
Alternatively, bake in a preheated oven at 200°C (400°F) for 30-35 minutes, turning halfway, until cooked.

cooking_tips: Marinating for a longer duration enhances the smoky flavor and tenderness.
Do not grill on very high heat initially; start with medium to cook through, then finish on higher heat for char.
Baste with any leftover marinade (boiled) or butter during the last few minutes of cooking.
serving_suggestions: Serve hot with a side of grilled vegetables, coleslaw, or potato salad.
A sprinkle of fresh coriander adds freshness.

name: Tandoori Chicken Recipe
short_description: Classic Indian tandoori chicken, marinated in yogurt and spices, then grilled.
full_description: Recreate the magic of a tandoor in your kitchen with this traditional Tandoori Chicken recipe. Qureshi's Chicken Tandoori Masala gives it an irresistible smoky flavor and vibrant color.

ingredients.text: 500g chicken pieces (thighs, drumsticks, or breast), skin removed
1/2 tsp red chili powder (for color, optional)
Lemon wedges and mint chutney for serving

preparation_steps.text: Make deep slits on the chicken pieces. In a large bowl, combine yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, Qureshi's Chicken Tandoori Masala, red chili powder (if using), and salt.
Add chicken to the marinade, ensuring each piece is thoroughly coated and marinade gets into the slits. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight.
Preheat oven to 220°C (425°F) or grill to medium-high heat. Lightly grease a baking tray or grill grates.
Place marinated chicken on the tray/grill. Bake/grill for 25-30 minutes, turning halfway and basting with oil/butter, until chicken is cooked through and charred at edges.

cooking_tips: For the authentic tandoor flavor, use a barbecue with charcoal.
The longer the marination, the more flavorful and tender the chicken will be.
Don't skip the basting step; it keeps the chicken moist and adds flavor.
serving_suggestions: Serve hot with onion rings, lemon wedges, and green mint chutney.
Accompaniments like naan bread or a fresh kachumber salad are perfect.

name: Traditional Sambhar Recipe
short_description: A comforting and flavorful South Indian lentil and vegetable stew.
full_description: Prepare a wholesome and delicious Sambhar, the quintessential South Indian stew, with Qureshi's Sambhar Masala. Packed with vegetables and lentils, it's nutritious and incredibly flavorful.

ingredients.text: 1/2 cup Toor Dal (split pigeon peas), washed
2 cups mixed vegetables (drumsticks, pumpkin, brinjal, carrots), chopped

preparation_steps.text: Cook Toor Dal with turmeric and water in a pressure cooker until soft and mushy. Mash lightly and set aside.
In a separate pot, cook mixed vegetables with a little water and salt until tender but still firm.
Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds, dry red chilies, asafoetida, and curry leaves. Let them splutter.
Add chopped onion and sauté until translucent. Add chopped tomato and cook until soft.
Stir in Qureshi's Sambhar Masala and cook for 1 minute.
Add cooked dal, cooked vegetables, and tamarind extract. Add water to achieve desired consistency. Bring to a boil and simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Adjust salt and serve hot.

cooking_tips: Use a variety of vegetables for a more nutritious and flavorful sambhar.
Adjust the tanginess by varying the amount of tamarind extract.
A fresh tempering at the end enhances the aroma and taste.
serving_suggestions: Serve hot with idli, dosa, vada, or steamed rice.
A dollop of ghee on top of hot sambhar is highly recommended.
